was procured by The Press. 1t was
for writing this letter that Noble was
indicted yesterday.


L. A. Perrot was manager of the
Washington Employment agency, took
an order for three girls for the
imaginary Edith Edwards at Wallace
and likewise wrote letter. This let
ter was also obtained by The. Press.


In all the cases which have result
ed in the punishment of these men
The Press furnished the evidence
Before the crusade was begun BY
this paper nearly all of the employ
ment agencies then In the city were
engaged in inducing young girls to
join the groups of fallen women
Through the efforts Of The Press all
Of the scoundrels have been compell-
ed to abandon their practices, and the
conducting of an employment agency
has again become a legitimate busi-
ness.


The city police made no effort to
stop the business. 1t was all done by
The Press and the United States of
ficials.


Mayor Boyd said no employment
agent in the city would take an order
tot place girls in house of i'll fame,
because he knew them to be men and
one had told him that he was a nice
man and would not do anything bad.
